input_text: Guidelines for a treatment of haemophilia
NL_requirements: After diagnosing the patient, the system must refer the patient to a Comprehensive Care Centre (CCC).
After administering treatment, the system must document the treatment process.
After administering treatment, the system must document the treatment outcome.
If the patient is a child with inhibitors and immune tolerance induction (ITI) has failed, the system must provide bypassing agents.
The system must not administer treatment if the factor quantity is less than 4 units per capita.
If gene therapy is used, the system must monitor factor expression.
After administering treatment, the system must evaluate chronic pain as a core outcome.
After administering treatment, the system must assess the mental health of the patient.
After administering treatment, the system must monitor resource use.
The activity to diagnose the patient must be executed by a specialist.
AST_requirements: condition_eventually_follows(tree, '(patientAge < 18) and (inhibitorsPresent == true) and (itiFailed == true)', 'provide bypassingAgents')

Modified Recommendations on clotting factor concentrates:
(128) Adequate amounts of coagulation factor concentrates for the treatment of 
patients with hemophilia and related disorders should be available in each country. 
There is a continuing need for both plasma-derived and recombinant products. At 
national level, the minimum acceptable level of factor concentrate use 
should be

VIII and IX [42,43].
